name: Alkane/oil sensor (AlkS + CRISPRa)
shortDescription: Detects medium-chain alkanes (diesel / crude-oil contamination). The AlkS regulator gates a CRISPRa circuit driving a fluorescent reporter, in the solvent-tolerant chassis P. putida KT2440.

input.detects: Alkane/oil contamination in soil and water (spill monitoring)

sensing.sensorMechanism: AlkS binds alkanes and activates the PalkB promoter of the alkane-degradation operon.
sensing.signalFlow: Alkane -> AlkS activates PalkB -> transcribes an sgRNA -> dCas9-activator amplifies a fluorescent reporter (CRISPRa) -> fluorescence rises with hydrocarbon contamination.

parts.name: AlkS regulator
parts.source: Pseudomonas putida/oleovorans OCT plasmid alk operon

parts.name: Reporter-activating sgRNA
parts.source: designed for CRISPRa upstream of a weak reporter promoter

performance.notes: AlkS is a classic alkane sensor and PalkB a widely used inducible promoter; integrated CRISPRa device is a design template. P. putida natively tolerates solvents.

safety.biocontainment: P. putida KT2440 certified HV1 safety host; add containment for field use.
safety.notes: Solvent-tolerant chassis suited to spill sites.

buildSteps.title: Design CRISPRa sgRNA
buildSteps.description: Target a weak reporter promoter; check host off-targets.

buildSteps.title: Assemble units
buildSteps.description: TU1: AlkS + PalkB -> sgRNA. TU2: dCas9-omega. TU3: weak promoter -> sfGFP. SEVA vectors.

buildSteps.title: Expose to sample
buildSteps.description: Add soil eluate/water + alkane standard curve.

provenance.source: Design template combining the AlkS alkane-sensing module with P. putida CRISPRa
partsSources: de Lorenzo et al. / AlkS-PalkB alkane regulation (Pseudomonas)
provenance.license: Parts per their original sources; design template CC BY 4.0
